title: lathyrus bolanderi subsp. violaceus
intro: Lathyrus bolanderi subsp. violaceus, also known as the purple peavine or violet pea-vine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is native to the Pacific coast of North America, where it grows in open woods and along streams. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. The purple peavine can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or dried and used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
